title: Integration Testing
content:
Integration tests live in the tests/ directory at the project root (alongside src/). Each file in tests/ is compiled as a separate crate that depends on your library. This means integration tests can only access your public API, testing your code the way external users would.

Integration tests verify that multiple components work together correctly. They test the public interface and can catch issues that unit tests miss, like incorrect module visibility or broken API contracts.

codeExamples.description: Shared test utilities in a common module

keyPoints: Integration tests live in tests/ directory, each file is a separate crate
They can only access your public API (pub items)
Each test file is compiled independently (useful for parallel testing)
Use tempfile crate for test files that auto-cleanup
Run specific test files: cargo test --test integration_test
